Title: The SlGRAS9-SlMYC1 regulatory module controls glandular trichome formation and modulates resilience to pest in tomato

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Volatile terpenoids (including α-humulene, β-caryophyllene, d-elemene, α-pinene, α-terpinene, γ-terpinene)
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contribute to resistance against herbivorous pests such as spider mites and aphids
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negative regulator; knockout increases type VI glandular trichome density and elevates terpenoid accumulation in leaves, sepals, petals, and fruits, enhancing pest tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Represses SlMYC1 transcription by direct promoter binding; indirectly suppresses TPS gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Type VI glandular trichome formation and associated terpenoid biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Upstream repressor module component; loss of function markedly increases trichome-associated terpenoid output across multiple organs
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: No interaction detected with Wo; Hair; SlCycB2; SlMYC1 in luciferase complementation assays
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in type VI glandular trichomes and trichome-rich tissues, highest in sepals; lower in trichome-rich accessions and higher in trichome-poor accessions
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in trichomes from leaves, stems, fruits, and sepals
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter used for overexpression assays in tobacco dual-luciferase system; native SlMYC1 promoter as reporter target
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; RT-qPCR; GC-MS metabolite profiling; dual-luciferase assay; EMSA; yeast one-hybrid; genetic interaction via double mutant analysis; pest bioassays
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptomic profiling from prior published trichome/leaf dataset (Balcke et al., 2017); DAP-seq evidence from prior study (Shi et al., 2024); no accession reported in this paper
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Spider mite infestation; aphid infestation; greenhouse growth under controlled conditions
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Slight reduction of long trichomes (types I/II/III) in fruits and sepals in knockout plants; no yield penalty reported
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising editing target for increasing glandular trichome density, terpenoid-mediated pest resistance, and crop resilience; knockout or down-regulation could be deployed alone or with positive regulators such as SlMYC1, with attention to possible shifts in trichome type composition
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlGRAS9 is highly expressed in trichomes and negatively correlated with trichome density across tomato accessions. 2. SlGRAS9 knockout increases type VI glandular trichomes and volatile terpenoids in leaves, sepals, petals, and fruits. 3. SlGRAS9 directly binds the SlMYC1 promoter and represses its transcription. 4. SlGRAS9 acts upstream of SlMYC1, and its loss enhances resistance to spider mites and aphids.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Tomato homolog of Arabidopsis SCL8; regulation of TPS genes is indirect and depends at least partly on SlMYC1; not itself shown to bind TPS promoters functionally in dual-luciferase assays

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Volatile terpenoids (including α-humulene, β-caryophyllene, d-elemene, α-pinene, α-terpinene, γ-terpinene)
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defensive specialized metabolites that improve tolerance to spider mites and aphids
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive regulator; overexpression increases type VI glandular trichome density and terpenoid accumulation, whereas knockout strongly reduces terpenoids to near-undetectable levels
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: TPS genes including SlTPS1; SlTPS5; SlTPS12; previously also linked to SlTPS9 and SlTPS20
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Positively regulates TPS gene expression, likely indirectly; previously shown to activate SlTPS1, SlTPS5, and SlTPS12 transcription
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Type VI glandular trichome development and terpenoid biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential positive regulator of the type VI trichome state that is required for high terpenoid accumulation across multiple tissues
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Repressed by SlGRAS9 via direct promoter binding
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Previously reported to form a regulatory module with Wo to activate TPS genes; in this paper no interaction with SlGRAS9 detected
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher in tomato accessions with high trichome density; increased in young leaves and fruit trichomes of Slgras9-CR plants
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected in young leaves and fruit trichomes; elevated in trichome-rich cultivars
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; RT-qPCR; scanning electron microscopy; GC-MS metabolite profiling; genetic interaction via double mutant analysis; pest bioassays
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: No new omics accession reported in this paper
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Spider mite infestation; aphid infestation; greenhouse growth under controlled conditions
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Knockout causes severe loss of type VI glandular trichomes and large reduction in defensive terpenoids; overexpression-associated growth penalties not reported
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for overexpression or activation editing to increase glandular trichomes, terpenoid-based defense, and pest resilience; may be combined with SlGRAS9 down-regulation for stronger effect
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlMYC1 overexpression increases type VI glandular trichomes in leaves, sepals, petals, and fruits. 2. SlMYC1 overexpression elevates terpenoid accumulation, while knockout reduces terpenoids to near-undetectable levels. 3. SlMYC1 acts downstream of SlGRAS9, as double mutants resemble Slmyc1 knockout. 4. Increased SlMYC1 activity improves resistance to spider mites and aphids.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: A previously established trichome regulator, experimentally central in this study through overexpression, knockout, and epistasis analyses; regulation of TPS genes is likely indirect
